extraction.one_line_summary: CICCONE Giulio attacked on the steep early ramps of the 22km final climb, splitting and dropping the break to solo victory.
tactical_patterns: Break formation delayed 100km by crashes/attacks; INEOS allowed weak GC threat break knowing energy expenditure would limit stage threat.
CICCONE exploited steep bottom section of 22km climb rather than attacking near summit, gaining decisive advantage before shallower gradients.
MARTIN Guillaume gained GC time from breakaway despite being outside top-5, demonstrating GC teams' focus fatigue on multi-climb stages.
CARTHY Hugh showed resilience fighting back after being dropped, suggesting endurance over explosive power on long final climbs.

rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: First win since pre-pandemic after lean years suggests form peak timing; dominated on steep early gradients of long climb.
rider_signals.BALMAN Kun: negative: Took KOM jersey by winning first climb but dropped immediately on harder Verania, revealing one-dimensional climber profile.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: Dropped then clawed back to front group twice but ultimately distanced; durable but lacks punch for steep pitches.
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: positive: Opportunistically gained 2+ minutes on GC group from break despite lower initial position, showing teams missed secondary threats.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: INEOS controlling but Carapaz involved in early crash; team burning energy defending pink rather than attacking.
